item.title: OpenAI adds open source tools to help developers build for teen safety
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Rather than working from scratch to figure out how to make AI safer for teens, developers can use these policies to fortify what they build.

item.title: Talat’s AI meeting notes stay on your machine, not in the cloud
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The subscription-free AI meeting notes app is a local-first twist on notetaking tools like Granola.

item.title: Doss raises $55M for AI inventory management that plugs into ERP
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Doss's AI-powered inventory management system integrates with existing ERP systems. The Series B round was co-led by Madrona and Premji Invest.

item.title: Meet the former Apple designer building a new AI interface at Hark
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The company said it would design models, hardware, and interfaces in tandem to deliver a "seamless end-to-end personal intelligence product."

item.title: Mirage raises $75M to continue building models for its AI video-editing app Captions
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Mirage, the maker of video-editing app Captions, has raised $75 million in growth financing from General Catalyst's Customer Value Fund (CVF).

item.title: Agile Robots becomes the latest robotics company to partner with Google DeepMind
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: Agile Robots will incorporate Google DeepMind's robotics foundation models into its bots while collecting data for the AI research lab.

item.title: Air Street becomes one of the largest solo VCs in Europe with $232M fund
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: London’s Air Street Capital has raised a large Fund III with eyes locked on backing early-stage European and North American AI companies.

item.title: Bernie Sanders’ AI ‘gotcha’ video flops, but the memes are great
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders thinks he's tricked Claude into revealing the AI industry's secrets, but he really just exposed how agreeable chatbots can become.

item.title: Vibe-coding startup Lovable is on the hunt for acquisitions
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Lovable's founder said the fast-growing vibe-coding startup is looking for startups and teams to join its company.

item.title: Apple sets June date for WWDC 2026, teasing ‘AI advancements’
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Apple will host its next Worldwide Developers Conference the week of June 8. The company is expected to announce major updates to Siri with advanced AI capabilities.

item.title: Startup Gimlet Labs is solving the AI inference bottleneck in a surprisingly elegant way
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Gimlet Labs just raised an $80 million Series A for tech that lets AI run across NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, ARM, Cerebras and d-Matrix chips, simultaneously.

item.title: Littlebird raises $11M for its AI-assisted ‘recall’ tool that reads your computer screen
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Littlebird is building an AI that reads your screen in real time to capture context, answer questions, and automate tasks, without relying on screenshots.

item.title: Elizabeth Warren calls Pentagon’s decision to bar Anthropic ‘retaliation’
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: In a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) equated the DOD's decision to label Anthropic a "supply-chain risk" as retaliation, arguing that the Pentagon could simply have terminated its contract with the AI lab.

item.title: Sam Altman-backed fusion startup Helion in talks to sell power to OpenAI
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is stepping down as board chair of Helion. His departure comes as reports that the two companies are negotiating a deal that would see Helion sell 12.5% of its power output to OpenAI.

item.title: Do you want to build a robot snowman?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we recapped CEO Jensen Huang’s GTC keynote and debated what it means for Nvidia’s future.

item.title: Cursor admits its new coding model was built on top of Moonshot AI’s Kimi
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Building on top of a Chinese model feels particularly fraught right now.

item.title: Elon Musk unveils chip manufacturing plans for SpaceX and Tesla
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Elon Musk recently outlined ambitious plans for a chip-building collaboration Tesla and SpaceX — but he has a history of overpromising.

item.title: Delve accused of misleading customers with ‘fake compliance’
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: An anonymous Substack post accuses compliance startup Delve of “falsely” convincing “hundreds of customers they were compliant” with privacy and security regulations.

item.title: An exclusive tour of Amazon’s Trainium lab, the chip that’s won over Anthropic, OpenAI, even Apple
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Shortly after Amazon announced its $50 billion investment in OpenAI, AWS invited me on a private tour of the chip lab at the heart of the deal.

item.title: Are AI tokens the new signing bonus or just a cost of doing business?
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Maybe tokens really will become the fourth pillar of engineering compensation. But engineers might want to hold the line before embracing this as a straightforward win.
